title: Formatting & Linting
description: Keep your code perfectly formatted and bug-free with rustfmt and clippy.

slides.title: The Rust Way 🧹
slides.content:
Rust developers care a lot about keeping code clean and readable.

Instead of arguing with your team over spaces vs tabs, Rust comes with an official tool to do it for you automatically: **rustfmt**.

slides.title: Auto-magic Formatting ✨
slides.content:
# `cargo fmt`

By simply running `cargo fmt` in your terminal, Rust will re-format every single code file in your project to follow the standard style guidelines.

No more messy code!

quizData.question: Which tool automatically formats your Rust code to match the community standard?
quizData.explanation: `cargo fmt` invokes `rustfmt` to format your entire project consistently.

slides.title: Meet Clippy 📎
slides.content:
Even if your code format is perfect, you might write code that is unoptimized or prone to errors.

Enter **Clippy**! Clippy is Rust's official linter. It acts like an incredibly smart code reviewer.

slides.title: Linter Extraordinaire 🧐
slides.content:
Run `cargo clippy` in your terminal.

Clippy will scan your code and provide warnings, tips, and even the exact code fixes to make your Rust code more idiomatic (which means 'written the correct Rust way').

quizData.question: What is the purpose of Cargo Clippy?

options.text: To analyze your code and suggest improvements/catch minor bugs
To compile your code into a binary

quizData.explanation: Clippy is a collection of lints to catch common mistakes and improve your Rust code.
